parentQuestion: Which of the following is the mildest consequence of traumatic brain injury?

scaffolds.q: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) can range from mild to severe.
scaffolds.explain: TBI severity exists on a spectrum, from mild injuries like concussions (temporary dysfunction, no visible structural damage) to severe injuries with bleeding, brain swelling, permanent damage, or death. Set out toward this: through this gradient of damage.

scaffolds.q: A concussion always shows visible damage on a brain CT scan.
scaffolds.explain: Concussions are functional injuries — the brain doesn't work right temporarily, but there's no structural damage visible on CT or MRI. This is what makes them 'mild' TBI — the tissue isn't torn, but the circuits are scrambled. Let me show you what this means.
